factors.summary: Free cash flow decreased compared to the previous period, primarily due to working capital changes.

evidence.text: Cash flows from operating activities decreased $1.1 billion in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to working capital changes driven by a decrease in accrued incentive compensation and other liabilities and an increase in accounts receivable, partially offset by an increase in accounts payable from the nine months of 2024.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow decreased compared to the previous period, primarily due to working capital changes.

factors.summary: The company has maintained its credit agreements and has not increased its debt leverage.

evidence.text: The Three-Year Credit Agreement and the Five-Year Credit Agreement expire in March 2027 and March 2029, respectively. Each of the Credit Agreements has a $125 million letter of credit sublimit. The Credit Agreements are available to finance our operations and other cash flow needs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company has maintained its credit agreements and has not increased its debt leverage.

factors.summary: Management has not provided specific forward guidance but has outlined potential risks and uncertainties that could impact future results.

evidence.text: As a result of these and other factors, no assurance can be given as to our future results and achievements. Accordingly, a forward-looking statement is neither a prediction nor a guarantee of future events or circumstances and those future events or circumstances may not occur.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Management has not provided specific forward guidance but has outlined potential risks and uncertainties that could impact future results.

factors.summary: Management identifies several key sensitivities including economic conditions, fuel prices, competition, and regulatory changes.

evidence.text: • economic conditions in the global markets in which we operate; [...] • the effect of intense competition on our ability to maintain or increase our prices [...] • the effects of global climate change; [...] • the effect of costs related to lawsuits [...] • increasing costs related to changing and heightened regulations and enforcement related to data protection;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management identifies several key sensitivities including economic conditions, fuel prices, competition, and regulatory changes.

factors.summary: The company faces potential litigation exposure, particularly related to disputes over employment classifications and other legal matters.

evidence.text: • any liability resulting from and the costs of defending against class-action, derivative, and other litigation, such as wage-and-hour, joint employment, securities, vehicle accident, and discrimination and retaliation claims, claims related to our reporting and disclosure of environmental and sustainability topics, and any other legal or governmental proceedings, including the matters discussed in Note 10 of the accompanying unaudited condensed consolidated financial statements;

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces potential litigation exposure, particularly related to disputes over employment classifications and other legal matters.

factors.summary: Environmental regulations are a concern, particularly regarding carbon neutrality goals and climate change.

sentiment.label: negative

evidence.text: • the price and availability of jet and vehicle fuel;

sentiment.rationale: Input costs such as fuel prices are a significant concern and could impact the company's ability to maintain profitability.

evidence.text: • our ability to successfully implement our business strategy and effectively respond to changes in market dynamics and customer preferences; [...] • our ability to successfully implement the planned tax-free spin-off of the FedEx Freight business [...] • our ability to execute and effectively operate, integrate, leverage, and grow acquired businesses, [...] • our ability to manage our network capacity and cost structure for capital expenditures and operating expenses, and match it to shifting and future customer volume levels;

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is focused on cost reduction and efficiency through initiatives like Network 2.0 and workforce reduction in Europe.

evidence.text: • significant changes in the volumes of shipments transported through our networks, customer demand for our various services, or the prices we obtain for our services;

factors.summary: Organic growth is dependent on successful business strategy implementation and market responses, with no specific indication of acceleration or deceleration.

evidence.text: • failure to successfully implement our business strategy and effectively respond to changes in market dynamics and customer preferences;

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are a focus, particularly through initiatives like Network 2.0, but challenges remain.

evidence.text: • achieve the expected operational efficiencies and network flexibility, alignment of our cost base with demand, cost savings and reductions to our permanent cost structure, and other benefits while managing the potential risks;

factors.summary: Capital expenditure trajectory is tied to managing network capacity and cost structure alignment with demand, with no specific indication of increase or decrease.

evidence.text: • our ability to manage our network capacity and cost structure for capital expenditures and operating expenses, and match it to shifting and future customer volume levels;

factors.summary: Debt leverage is a concern, particularly regarding maintaining credit ratings and meeting financial covenants.

evidence.text: • constraints, volatility, or disruption in the capital markets, our ability to maintain our current credit ratings, commercial paper ratings, and senior unsecured debt and pass-through certificate credit ratings, and our ability to meet credit agreement financial covenants;

evidence.text: • the effects of evolving or new U.S. domestic or international government regulations, laws, policies, and actions, which could be unfavorable to our business, including labor [...] • increasing costs, the volatility of costs and funding requirements, and other legal mandates for employee benefits, especially pension and healthcare benefits;

factors.summary: Potential catalysts include successful business strategy implementation, tax-free spin-off of FedEx Freight, and achieving carbon neutrality goals.

evidence.text: • our ability to successfully implement our business strategy and effectively respond to changes in market dynamics and customer preferences; [...] • our ability to achieve or demonstrate progress on our goal of carbon neutrality for our global operations by calendar 2040;

evidence.text: • economic conditions in the global markets in which we operate; [...] • the effect of intense competition on our ability to maintain or increase our prices [...] • the effects of global climate change; [...] • the effect of costs related to lawsuits [...] • increasing costs related to changing and heightened regulations and enforcement related to data protection;

evidence.text: • our ability to achieve or demonstrate progress on our goal of carbon neutrality for our global operations by calendar 2040; [...] • our ability to successfully implement our workforce reduction in Europe;

factors.summary: Management's confidence is reflected in their focus on strategic initiatives and cost management, though uncertainties exist.

evidence.text: • our ability to successfully implement our business strategy and effectively respond to changes in market dynamics and customer preferences; [...] • our ability to execute and effectively operate, integrate, leverage, and grow acquired businesses, [...] • our ability to manage our network capacity and cost structure for capital expenditures and operating expenses, and match it to shifting and future customer volume levels;

factors.summary: The company is planning a spin-off of FedEx Freight, which could be a significant strategic move.

evidence.text: In December 2024, we announced that FedEx’s Board of Directors decided to pursue a full separation of FedEx Freight through the capital markets, creating a new publicly traded company.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Spin-off of FedEx Freight is a significant strategic move.

factors.summary: Higher interest rates are negatively impacting demand and thus hurting the company.

evidence.text: Elevated inflation and interest rates are negatively affecting consumer and business spending.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Higher rates are negatively impacting demand.

factors.summary: The company is investing in digital technologies and automation through initiatives like Network 2.0.

evidence.text: We have implemented Network 2.0 optimization in more than 200 locations in the U.S. and Canada. Contracted service providers will handle the pickup and delivery of Federal Express packages in some locations while employee couriers will handle others.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Investment in digital tech and automation through Network 2.0.

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, such as the invalidation of a tax regulation, are creating opportunities but also involve ongoing litigation risks.

evidence.text: We are currently under examination by the Internal Revenue Service for the 2016 through 2021 tax years. It is reasonably possible that certain income tax return proceedings will be completed during the next 12 months and could result in a change in our balance of unrecognized tax benefits. However, we believe we have recorded adequate amounts of tax, including interest and penalties, for any adjustments expected to occur.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Opportunities and ongoing risks from regulatory changes.

factors.summary: The company faces significant litigation exposure related to tax regulations, which could impact financial results if unresolved.

evidence.text: We filed suit in U.S. District Court for the Western District of Tennessee challenging the validity of a tax regulation related to the one-time transition tax on foreign earnings not repatriated... We continue to work towards obtaining a final judgment for the applicable refund amounts due to the regulation being invalid. Once the District Court enters a final judgment, the U.S. government could file an appeal with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit. If we are ultimately unsuccessful in defending our position, we may be required to reverse the benefit previously recorded.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant litigation exposure affecting financials.

factors.summary: Management has maintained forward guidance, anticipating continued pressure from macroeconomic conditions but expecting to mitigate some impacts through cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: In the remainder of 2025, we expect revenue to continue to be pressured by macroeconomic conditions... We will continue to execute on our DRIVE program initiatives focused on reducing our permanent cost structure, aligning our cost base with demand, and increasing the flexibility of our network.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Anticipation of continued pressure with cost reduction plans.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, though there was a failed pilot agreement ratification, which did not impact operations.

evidence.text: In July 2023, Federal Express’s pilots failed to ratify the tentative successor agreement... The ongoing bargaining process has no effect on our operations.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Labor relations are stable despite a failed agreement, with no operational impact.

factors.summary: Demand trends are negative, with deceleration observed across both domestic and international markets, particularly impacting the transportation industry and international trade.

evidence.text: The decline in U.S. imports of consumer goods that started in late 2022, along with slowed global industrial production, has contributed to weakened business conditions for the transportation industry.

In the remainder of 2025, we expect revenue to continue to be pressured by macroeconomic conditions, including uncertainty related to international trade, negatively affecting customer demand and constraining yield growth.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative demand trends across both domestic and international markets impacting transportation and trade.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditures are expected to decrease slightly compared to the previous year, signaling a cautious approach to future growth investments.

evidence.text: Our capital expenditures for 2025 are expected to be approximately $4.9 billion, $0.3 billion lower than 2024, as we continue to reduce our capital intensity relative to revenue.
Our capital expenditures for 2025 are expected to be approximately $4.9 billion, $0.3 billion lower than 2024, as we continue to reduce our capital intensity relative to revenue.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Slight decrease in capex suggests cautious investment approach, neither positive nor negative trend.

factors.summary: The company is repurchasing shares, signaling confidence, but the pace may be influenced by market conditions.

evidence.text: We repurchased an aggregate of $497 million of our common stock in the third quarter of 2025 through open market transactions. For the nine months of 2025, we completed $2.5 billion in share repurchases through ASR and open market transactions.

We repurchased an aggregate of $497 million of our common stock through open market transactions during the third quarter of 2025... As of February 28, 2025, $2.6 billion remained available to be used for repurchases under the 2024 stock repurchase program.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Share repurchases indicate confidence in the company's future despite potential market influences.

factors.summary: The company exhibits mixed performance in pricing power, with challenges in maintaining prices due to macroeconomic factors and inflation, yet successfully increasing prices in specific segments like U.S. domestic package and freight segments.

evidence.text: We are experiencing pressure on demand for our transportation services, particularly our priority services, as elevated inflation and interest rates are negatively affecting consumer and business spending.
U.S. domestic composite package yield increased slightly in both the third quarter and nine months of 2025 primarily due to higher base rates from our continued focus on revenue quality.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance with challenges and some successes in specific segments.

factors.summary: The company's operating margins are under significant pressure, showing a negative trajectory due to various factors such as economic conditions, cost pressures, lower fuel surcharges, and higher labor costs. While there was a slight improvement in the third quarter, the overall trend over nine months reflects a decline.

evidence.text: Our cash and cash equivalents, cash from operations, and available financing sources will be adequate to meet our liquidity needs, which include operational requirements, expected capital expenditures, voluntary pension contributions, dividend payments, and stock repurchases. In the third quarter of 2025, we began incurring costs and expenses related to the planned spin-off of FedEx Freight, which are expected to be significant but will not materially adversely affect our liquidity.

Operating margin decreased 90 basis points to 5.2% in the nine months of 2025 compared to 6.1% in the prior year period, driven by lower fuel surcharges, increased labor costs, and other operational challenges.

Federal Express segment operating income increased 10% in the third quarter of 2025 due to higher base yields and volume, partially offset by increased operating expenses. Federal Express segment operating income decreased 6% in the nine months of 2025 due to increased operating expenses and one fewer operating day, partially offset by higher base yields and volume.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant pressure on operating margins due to multiple adverse factors.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is showing a dual trend, with an increase in demand for higher-margin U.S. ground and international export services, which positively impacts operating income, while simultaneously experiencing a shift towards lower-margin international economy packages, negatively affecting yields.

evidence.text: Operating income for the third quarter and nine months of 2025 was positively affected by continued savings related to DRIVE, increased transportation segment base yields, and higher demand for U.S. ground and international export package services.
International export composite package yield decreased 4% in the third quarter and 3% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to unfavorable service mix.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Dual trends in revenue mix with positive and negative impacts.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across the company, driven by factors such as lower fuel surcharges, unfavorable currency exchange rates, and decreased volume in certain segments. While the Federal Express segment experienced some growth, it also faced challenges like reduced freight volume and currency impacts.

evidence.text: Revenue increased 2% in the third quarter and was flat in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to base yield improvement at both of our transportation segments and higher volume at Federal Express, offset by lower volume and fuel surcharges at FedEx Freight and unfavorable currency exchange rates.

Federal Express segment revenue increased 3% in the third quarter and 1% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to increased deferred and U.S. ground package volume and improved yields, partially offset by decreases from lower priority package and U.S. freight volume and unfavorable currency exchange rates.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decelerating organic growth driven by several unfavorable factors.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel prices, are declining, which is helping to mitigate some cost pressures and providing some relief. However, the declines are not sufficient to offset all challenges faced by the company.

evidence.text: During the third quarter and nine months of 2025, lower fuel prices negatively affected yields through lower fuel surcharges at FedEx Freight and reduced fuel expense at both of our transportation segments.
Fuel expense decreased 23% in the third quarter and 18% in the nine months of 2025 due to decreases in fuel prices and usage resulting from lower flight hours.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are declining but not enough to offset all challenges.

factors.summary: The transportation industry, particularly FedEx Freight, is experiencing a slowdown due to macroeconomic conditions such as reduced U.S. imports and global industrial production. However, there are signs of strength in the U.S. domestic market, particularly in package volume, while international markets, especially those tied to industrial economies, are showing softness.

evidence.text: The decline in U.S. imports of consumer goods that started in late 2022, along with slowed global industrial production, has contributed to weakened business conditions for the transportation industry. Consequently, this environment has led to lower shipments at FedEx Freight, negatively affecting our results in the third quarter and nine months of 2025.
U.S. domestic package volume increased, driven by deferred and ground services, while international priority package volume decreased due to softness in the global industrial economy.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The transportation industry is facing a slowdown due to reduced imports and global industrial production.

factors.summary: Volume trends show mixed performance across different segments. While Federal Express segment revenue increased due to higher U.S. ground and international economy package volumes, FedEx Freight experienced a decline in revenue and shipments, driven by macroeconomic conditions.

evidence.text: Federal Express segment revenue increased 3% in the third quarter and 1% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to increased deferred and U.S. ground package volume and improved base yields, offset by lower priority package volume.
FedEx Freight revenue decreased 5% in the third quarter and 6% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to lower shipments, fuel surcharges, and weight per shipment.

U.S. ground home delivery/economy package volume increased 11% in the third quarter and 4% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to increased demand for our services and, in the third quarter of 2025, the timing of cyber week.

International economy package volume increased 48% in the third quarter and 42% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to continued growth in our deferred service offerings as a result of strengthening e-commerce.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance across segments with some increases and declines.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs, such as DRIVE, are showing measurable savings but may face short-term lag. The initiatives are helping to offset cost pressures through various optimizations.

evidence.text: We have implemented Network 2.0 optimization in more than 200 locations in the U.S. and Canada... We expect savings from the plan to be between $125 million and $175 million on an annualized basis beginning in 2027.

These initiatives included the continued transformation of our structural network, improving the efficiency of our information technology and back-office functions, optimizing operations in Europe, and increasing linehaul efficiencies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are showing measurable savings and strategic optimizations.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher wage rates and staffing needs, creating margin pressure. The rise is attributed to increased staffing to meet higher volume and peak demand, as well as enhanced retirement benefits for more employees, though partially offset by savings initiatives.

evidence.text: Salaries and employee benefits expense increased 2% in the third quarter and 1% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to an increase in wage rates, increased staffing to align with higher volume, and an increase in retirement benefits due to changes in our deferred contribution plan that increased the number of eligible employees at Federal Express, partially offset by savings from our DRIVE initiatives and favorable currency exchange rates.

Salaries and employee benefits expense increased 4% in the third quarter and 2% in the nine months of 2025 primarily due to an increase in wage rates, increased staffing to align with peak demand, and an increase in retirement benefits due to changes to our defined contribution plan which increased the number of eligible employees, partially offset by savings from our DRIVE initiatives.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher wages and benefits, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving overall due to initiatives like Network 2.0 and DRIVE, but there are challenges in certain regions like Europe where execution is slower.

evidence.text: We have implemented Network 2.0 optimization in more than 200 locations in the U.S. and Canada... The execution of the plan is subject to a consultation process that is expected to occur over an 18-month period in accordance with local country processes and regulations.

These initiatives included the continued transformation of our structural network, improving the efficiency of our information technology and back-office functions, optimizing operations in Europe, and increasing linehaul efficiencies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving with initiatives like Network 2.0 and DRIVE, despite regional challenges.

factors.summary: Management exhibits mixed confidence, with caution regarding macroeconomic challenges but proactive steps and strategic initiatives to mitigate impacts.

evidence.text: The Board of Directors approved a change in FedEx’s fiscal year end from May 31 to December 31... We are experiencing pressure on demand for our transportation services... We expect inflation and high interest rates to continue to negatively affect our results of operations for the remainder of 2025.
We will continue to execute on our DRIVE program initiatives focused on reducing our permanent cost structure, aligning our cost base with demand, and increasing the flexibility of our network.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed confidence with cautious remarks about macroeconomic challenges but proactive steps.

factors.summary: The near-term risks are predominantly negative, encompassing geopolitical tensions, inflation, trade policy changes, macroeconomic instability, and contract expirations that could affect revenue and costs.

evidence.text: Geopolitical conflicts may adversely affect our business and results of operations... The changes in trade policy discussed above under 'Macroeconomic Conditions' could exacerbate global inflation.

The uncertainty of a slowdown in the global economy, global inflation, geopolitical challenges, developments in international trade, and the effects these factors will have on the rate of growth of global trade, supply chains, fuel prices, and our business in particular, make any expectations for the remainder of 2025 inherently less certain.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks are predominantly negative, including geopolitical tensions, inflation, and macroeconomic instability.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategy is focused on optimizing operations through initiatives like Network 2.0 and the FedEx Freight spin-off, while also reducing capital intensity and investing in strategic projects to maintain competitiveness.

evidence.text: We have implemented Network 2.0 optimization in more than 200 locations in the U.S. and Canada... In December 2024, we announced that FedEx’s Board of Directors decided to pursue a full separation of FedEx Freight through the capital markets, creating a new publicly traded company.

Our capital expenditures for 2025 are expected to be approximately $4.9 billion, $0.3 billion lower than 2024, as we continue to reduce our capital intensity relative to revenue. Aircraft spend is expected to decline, partially offset by increased investments in network optimization and modernization of our facilities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company's focus on optimizing operations and strategic investments suggests a proactive approach to maintaining competitiveness.

factors.summary: The planned spin-off of FedEx Freight is a key catalyst for future growth and strategic benefits, while the completion of ongoing tax litigation and cost reduction initiatives may also contribute to improved results. However, there is a risk that unresolved tax litigation could negatively impact the company's financial position.

evidence.text: The transaction, which would be implemented through the spin-off of shares of the new company to FedEx stockholders, is expected to be tax-free for U.S. federal income tax purposes for FedEx stockholders and be completed by June 2026.

Once the District Court enters a final judgment, the U.S. government could file an appeal with the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Sixth Circuit. If we are ultimately unsuccessful in defending our position, we may be required to reverse the benefit previously recorded.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Potential catalysts include successful strategy implementation and achieving sustainability goals, indicating opportunities.

factors.summary: The company faces significant sensitivities across multiple areas, including fuel prices, inflation, interest rates, geopolitical factors, global economic conditions, and currency exchange rates. These factors can have both positive and negative impacts on the company's operating results and demand for its transportation services, with notable risks posed by geopolitical conflicts and economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: The timing and amount of fluctuations in fuel prices and our ability to recover incremental fuel costs through our fuel surcharges can significantly affect our operating results either positively or negatively in the short-term...

Global inflation decelerated year-over-year but continues to be above historical levels. Additionally, global interest rates remained elevated in an effort to curb inflation. We are experiencing pressure on demand for our transportation services...
Geopolitical conflicts may adversely affect our business and results of operations.

The uncertainty of a slowdown in the global economy, global inflation, geopolitical challenges, developments in international trade, and the effects these factors will have on the rate of growth of global trade, supply chains, fuel prices, and our business in particular, make any expectations for the remainder of 2025 inherently less certain.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Multiple sensitivities like fuel prices and inflation pose significant risks to the company's operating results.

factors.summary: Currency exchange rates are negatively impacting revenue and positively impacting expenses, affecting margins.

evidence.text: Revenue for the nine months of 2025 was also negatively affected by one fewer operating day and reduced demand surcharges. Federal Express revenue for the nine months of 2025 was also negatively affected by one fewer operating day and unfavorable currency exchange rates.
Currency exchange rates had a negative effect on revenue and a positive effect on expenses and operating income in the third quarter and nine months of 2025.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Currency exchange rates are negatively impacting revenue and increasing expenses, affecting margins.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are acting as a significant headwind, with both the transportation industry and overall business facing challenges due to a slowdown and potential recession risks. The decline in U.S. imports and slowed global industrial production have weakened business conditions, and macroeconomic factors such as international trade uncertainty are negatively impacting demand and constraining revenue growth.

evidence.text: The transportation industry is highly cyclical and especially susceptible to trends in economic activity. Our primary business is to transport goods, so our business levels are directly tied to the purchase and production of goods and the rate of global trade growth. The decline in U.S. imports of consumer goods that started in late 2022, along with slowed global industrial production, has contributed to weakened business conditions for the transportation industry.

In the remainder of 2025, we expect revenue to continue to be pressured by macroeconomic conditions, including uncertainty related to international trade, negatively affecting customer demand and constraining yield growth.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Economic slowdowns and potential recessions are creating headwinds for transportation services and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are present and pose both direct and indirect challenges to the company. While the company does not expect direct material effects from specific conflicts, broader economic impacts and heightened other risks remain significant. Additionally, geopolitical uncertainties, such as trade tensions and international relations, contribute to economic slowdowns and supply chain disruptions, increasing operational and financial uncertainties.

evidence.text: Given the nature of our business and global operations, geopolitical conflicts may adversely affect our business and results of operations. While we do not expect ongoing geopolitical conflicts between Russia and Ukraine and in the Middle East to have a direct material effect on our business or results of operations, the broader consequences are adversely affecting the global economy and may also have the effect of heightening other risks disclosed in our Annual Report.

The uncertainty of a slowdown in the global economy, global inflation, geopolitical challenges, developments in international trade, and the effects these factors will have on the rate of growth of global trade, supply chains, fuel prices, and our business in particular, make any expectations for the remainder of 2025 inherently less certain.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Geopolitical uncertainties are negatively impacting the global economy and increasing operational risks.

factors.summary: The company is restructuring its workforce, particularly in Europe, as part of cost reduction initiatives. This restructuring is part of the DRIVE program and involves a workforce reduction plan impacting approximately 1,500 employees across back-office and commercial functions. The restructuring is expected to occur over an 18-month period, subject to local consultations.

evidence.text: In June 2024, Federal Express announced a workforce reduction plan in Europe as part of its ongoing measures to reduce structural costs. The plan will impact approximately 1,500 employees in Europe across back-office and commercial functions. The execution of the plan is subject to a consultation process that is expected to occur over an 18-month period in accordance with local country processes and regulations.
See the ‘Business Optimization Costs’ section of this MD&A for additional information on our DRIVE program, workforce reduction plan in Europe, and other cost savings initiatives.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Workforce restructuring indicates cost-cutting measures, which may signal underlying financial pressures or inefficiencies.
